digest.component_or_area: PyPI release/publish workflow and release-source authorization gate
propositions: The release workflow's only gate accepts any pushed v* tag without verifying the tagged commit is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary commit to be published.

The three version values in pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json.packages[].version are kept consistent only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing versions across PyPI, GitHub Release, and MCP channels.
Releases should be initiated only via workflow_dispatch from the main ref, guaranteeing the published source is reviewed-and-landed by construction.
A version-lockstep check must assert all manifest versions equal the version input and fail before build/tag creation on any mismatch.
Publication must go directly via OIDC trusted publishing with no intermediate tag relay or PAT, gated on a re-keyed pypi environment requiring reviewer approval.
The v* tag and GitHub Release should be created only after a successful publish, as a record output that never triggers publication.
